action.reasoning: Observation: inference-only TTA/calibration changes degraded the best score (0.564→0.541), so the current five-crop/logit-adjust path is not helping target generalization. Analysis: the strongest signal so far is that training-side augmentation improved target BA materially, while DANN and heavier inference tricks did not. The remaining weakness is likely source-domain overfitting plus class imbalance; a good next step is a stronger but still low-risk training recipe: mixup/cutmix-style regularization is awkward for balanced accuracy and camera traps, so instead I will add RandAugment + repeated augmentation via weighted sampling, use focal loss on top of balanced logits to emphasize hard/rare classes, and train a bit longer from the proven non-DANN architecture. I will keep predict.py simple/robust with center or flip TTA only. Decision: patch train.py to support balanced focal loss and stronger augmentation, train a moderate run starting from the best recipe, smoke test predict.py on val, and submit the new checkpoint.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 8 --batch_size 32 --lr 2e-5 --backbone_lr 8e-6 --label_smoothing 0.03 --strong_aug --use_focal --focal_gamma 1.5 --checkpoint_path checkpoints/focal_aug.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/focal_aug.pt --output smoke_val.json
